Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to construct an accepting Petri net for the described insurance claim process. The process includes activities: Claim Registration (CR), Document Verification (DV), Fraud Check (FC), Claim Assessment (CA), Approval (AP), Payment (P), Notification (N), Closure (CL). Additionally, there are loops: if documents incomplete after DV, a Re-Document Submission (RDS) step occurs before continuing. Similarly, if FC raises doubts, an additional In-depth Investigation (II) is performed, possibly multiple times before proceeding to CA.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We can model it as a Petri net with places representing the state before and after each activity, and transitions representing the activities. The loops can be modeled by adding places that allow multiple passes. Lets outline the process flow: Start place with initial token. Then Claim Registration (CR) occurs. After CR, go to Document Verification (DV). After DV, if documents are complete, proceed to Fraud Check (FC). If incomplete, go to Re-Document Submission (RDS) and then back to DV.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a basic Petri net without guards, we cannot encode conditions strictly, but since we are constructing an accepting Petri net, we can model the choice as two separate transitions from the same place After DV: one to FC and one to RDS. The actual path depends on the firing sequence, and as long as there exists a sequence that reaches the final marking, it is fine.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For the DV-RDS loop: After DV -> RDS -> After CR -> DV. For the FC-II loop: After FC -> II -> After DV -> FC. Places: Start, After CR, After DV, After FC, After CA, After AP, After P, After N, Closed. Transitions for all activities.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Arcs: Start -> CR -> After CR -> DV -> After DV -> FC -> After FC -> CA -> After CA -> AP -> After AP -> P -> After P -> N -> After N -> CL -> Closed. Also After DV -> RDS -> After CR (loop). After FC -> II -> After DV (loop).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Test a successful path: Start -> CR -> After CR -> DV -> After DV -> FC -> After FC -> CA -> After CA -> AP -> After AP -> P -> After P -> N -> After N -> CL -> Closed. Paths with loops also reach final marking, e.g., including RDS or II firings followed by correct choices.

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Considered separate places for complete/incomplete or additional transitions for decisions, but rejected due to lack of guards in basic Petri nets and simplicity needs.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Use direct arcs from RDS to After CR and from II to After DV to model loops simply, accepting potential non-determinism as sufficient for accepting Petri net semantics.
